---
authors:
- Anthony Wayne
collection_id: 1053
collections:
- Anthony Wayne Letterbooks Vol.1-3
created: '2018-07-16T14:21:31+00:00'
day: '05'
description: There are a number of vacancies in the officer corps due to resignation, court martial, and even one suicide. Wayne wants to know the correct procedure for filling these vacancies. Since it
  appears that everything is ready for movement down the Ohio River, he wants to know which troops should be left in garrison and recommends that those who are not combat-ready be assigned to that duty.
doc_types:
- Letterbook Copy
document_number: '1793040553555'
omeka_image_id: 22073

month: '04'
notable_items:
- Posts & Garrisons
- one Commissioned Officer for each boat
- General Court Martial
- report of the Court of Inquiry
- a copy of a letter from Major Bedinger with his resignation
- vacancies
- these casualties
- anxiety of ambition
- death of Capt. Howe [suicide]
notable_locations:
- Pittsburgh
- each county bordering on the Ohio
- the upper parts of the Ohio
- Fort Steuben

- Henry Knox
- Anthony Wayne
- scouts
- the Legion
- General Wilkinson
- Indians
